title: Privacy-Aware Corpus Intelligence Pipeline
summary: Local-first text intelligence platform that classifies a large conversational corpus into public-safe, private, and review queues using deterministic PII detection, sensitive-domain rules, Presidio, spaCy, and ensemble validation without relying on bulk LLM inference.
proof: Shows privacy-aware corpus ingestion, chunking, classifier agreement testing, ensemble routing, audit reports, and cost-conscious non-LLM text classification.
thought: We often hear that conversations may be recorded for audit, quality, or training purposes. I wanted to look behind that idea from a data engineering angle: how do you remove identity, sensitive context, and private identifiers from a large text archive while still preserving useful knowledge? This project became a practical privacy filtering pipeline that validates multiple non-LLM approaches and chooses an ensemble route that is inspectable, affordable, and realistic for 2026.

title: Failure-Aware Metric Realignment for Post-Hoc Dense Retrieval
summary: Built a local-first retrieval research system for evaluating whether post-hoc vector-space corrections can improve dense retrieval without retraining the base embedding model. The project focuses on reproducible corpus evaluation, failure analysis, metric comparison, and evidence-backed experiment artifacts so retrieval improvements can be measured across iterations instead of lost in one-off runs.
proof: Shows reproducible dense-retrieval experimentation, failure analysis, metric comparison, and evidence-backed local evaluation artifacts.
thought: Dense retrieval systems can look strong under aggregate metrics while still failing in important edge cases. This project focuses on making those failures visible and testing whether post-hoc vector-space corrections can improve retrieval quality without retraining the base embedding model.

title: Vector-Based Conflict Alert System Testbed
summary: Local-first conflict alert testbed with a seeded random traffic generator and a replayable radar visualization. Demonstrates deterministic physics-based screening, alert emission, and an inspection UI for how and when an aircraft pair becomes a conflict candidate.
proof: Shows a reproducible, seeded air-traffic simulation where alerting triggers only when the geometry becomes unsafe, with a UI surface that can replay and inspect the alert window.
thought: vCAS came from seeing repeated news about aircraft conflicts and thinking about how physics-driven alerting could support people working under extreme attention pressure. It is an exploration of simple, explainable vector math as a practical aid for safer traffic awareness.

title: FinLens: Regulatory-Grade Banking Data Platform
summary: Banking data platform for FDIC, FRED, and SEC public datasets with raw landing zones, dbt-modeled analytics tables, Airflow orchestration, data quality checks, dashboards, and documentation for regulatory and financial analysis.
proof: Shows end-to-end data platform thinking: ingestion, modeling, orchestration, validation, serving, documentation, and regulatory-style analytical workflows.

title: Privacy Preserving Authentication Audit Data Platform
summary: Local-first authentication audit data platform that ingests public login-event telemetry, validates the event contract, pseudonymizes sensitive identifiers, captures ingestion audit evidence, and exposes compact table previews through a documentation-driven project surface.
proof: Shows privacy-aware event ingestion, queue semantics, deterministic transformation, local reproducibility, and relational landing design.

title: Portfolio Assistant Gateway & Context Orchestrator
summary: Assistant and contact orchestration layer that powers the live portfolio site, making the website itself the production demonstration of the chat, routing, context preparation, and contact-intake workflow.
proof: Shows an assistant-backed public website where the interaction surface, contact capture, origin controls, session state, and operator handoff are part of the running product.

title: Agentic Planning and Execution Intelligence Platform
summary: Local-first planning and AI-operations system that turns scoped goals, retained feedback, and iterative model output into progressively sharper execution plans. The current build adds a model router, prompt registry, guardrails, and a local evaluation harness around the Overthinker planning loop.
proof: Shows feedback-driven planning iteration, prompt governance, model-call capture, guardrail validation, local evaluation evidence, and operator-readable execution artifacts.

title: Iterative Research Intelligence Workbench
summary: Local iterative research tool built around Ollama with a web UI and feedback loops that allow the operator to nudge research direction over time.
proof: Shows local-model orchestration, iterative research workflows, and human-in-the-loop refinement.
thought: In an age where AI is taking on more work, I wanted to explore whether it could also absorb part of the stress of overthinking. Deep Researcher is my attempt to make a system that stays with one problem through multiple passes, pressure-tests its own reasoning, and improves the answer instead of rushing to the first response.

title: Vehicle Intelligence Lakehouse
summary: Local-first vehicle telemetry lakehouse for live OBD/mobile payloads, profile-aware simulator events, and file-based replay under one session-aware contract. The backend persists raw and normalized telemetry through SQLite or PostgreSQL lakehouse tables, serves dashboard and live-stream APIs, and exposes schema/export endpoints for downstream consumers.
proof: Operational endpoint set includes live REST intake, session creation, profile APIs, simulator controls, dashboard endpoints, SSE streaming, Bronze/Silver/Gold transform paths, and signed/encrypted exchange contracts.
thought: Connected-vehicle analytics only become useful when the event contract is stable from raw intake to live display. This project treats simulator, mobile relay, replay, dashboard, and downstream export as consumers of the same trusted telemetry model.

title: Semantic Expert Routing Architecture
summary: Research-grade library for semantic mixture-of-experts architecture targeting small-compute environments, combining a lightweight transformer backbone with teacher-student routing and dynamic clustering.
proof: Shows deeper systems thinking around routing, specialization, and compute-aware model architecture.
thought: ASTRA-X Base came from a simple architectural curiosity: what if routing and specialization could be explored differently from the usual mixture-of-experts path. It is a small-compute research build around alternative model routing, specialization, and adaptive structure.

title: Local Assistant Orchestration Gateway
summary: Python service repository for the ASTRA-X ecosystem. Public metadata is limited, so this entry is intentionally framed as an endpoint/API component pending a fuller project writeup.
proof: Shows service-boundary work inside the broader ASTRA-X architecture.
thought: ASTRA-X EndPoint is about treating an AI system as a set of clean service boundaries instead of one large tangled script. The idea is to explore how model calls, APIs, runtime controls, and operator-facing interfaces can be separated into a maintainable endpoint layer.

title: Artificial Life Lineage Simulation Lab
summary: Local-first artificial life simulator where agents evolve, reproduce, compete, and form lineages over time with SQL-backed tick history, real-time dashboards, replayable timelines, and controllable world parameters.
proof: Shows event history modeling, simulation telemetry, replayability, and analytical UI design.
thought: LineageLab started as a thought experiment after playing Age of Empires: if a game can spawn a character, could we simulate a small world where survival changes with attributes, environment, and inherited behavior. It lets me explore ecosystem dynamics, lineage, replayable history, and analytics as a controllable miniature world.

title: Synthetic Credit Score Modeling
summary: Machine learning project using GAN-generated synthetic credit data for thin-file or no-file credit histories, with custom score prediction to improve inclusivity in credit assessment.
proof: Shows applied ML, synthetic data generation, and financial-domain modeling.
thought: SynthScoreNet is motivated by the real friction of arriving in a system where a thin or missing credit file can limit access. The project explores whether synthetic histories, fairer modeling, and imbalance-aware ML can support more inclusive credit assessment without reducing the problem to a blunt score.
